message:
test: pin err.h conformance via differential harness

Adds tests/conformance_diff_err_h.rs covering 3 functions:
- warnx: body matching for plain + format-args
- warn: body+errno-suffix matching across 5 errnos
- errx: fork-isolated exit code + body across 3 cases

6 tests pass.

Discovered DISC-ERR-001: fl reads progname from /proc/self/comm
(kernel TASK_COMM_LEN=15-byte truncation) while glibc reads argv[0]
basename. Compares only message body, documents the prefix mismatch;
bd-errh2 opened for the progname source fix.

Beads: bd-errh1 (harness), bd-errh2 (progname source)

files.patch:
@@ -65,6 +65,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1h4","title":"Guard: forbid todo!/unimplemented! in any ABI symbol marked Implemented","description":"Critique mapping: #3.

Deliverables:
- A lint (rg-based or Rust unit test) that:
  - finds todo!/unimplemented! in crates/glibc-rs-abi and crates/glibc-rs-core
  - cross-checks against support matrix

Acceptance:
- If a symbol is marked Implemented, it must not contain todo!/unimplemented! on any reachable path.
- If a symbol is Stub, it must return a deterministic errno and be documented.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:46.471737822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:50:08.304964Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T05:50:08.304964Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","critique","stubs"],"comments":[{"id":673,"issue_id":"bd-1h4","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. CI guard script: scripts/check_stub_guard.sh
Four checks:
1. No todo!/unimplemented!/panic! in ABI crate source
2. No reachable stubs for Implemented/RawSyscall symbols (via census cross-reference)
3. Support matrix consistency audit (11 informational warnings)
4. Stub errno contracts (exported Stub symbols must use deterministic errno, not todo!)

Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=reachable todo in Implemented, 2=matrix inconsistency, 3=stub contract violation.

### 2. Integration test: crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/stub_guard_test.rs
5 tests:
- no_todo_in_abi_crate: grep-free scan of ABI crate
- census_exists_and_no_reachable_stubs: parse census JSON, verify 0 reachable
- census_is_deterministic: run census twice, compare (excluding timestamp)
- support_matrix_exists_and_valid: schema validation of support_matrix.json
- implemented_symbols_have_abi_exports: cross-check matrix against actual ABI exports

### Test commands

running 5 tests
test census_exists_and_no_reachable_stubs ... ok
test no_todo_in_abi_crate ... ok
test support_matrix_exists_and_valid ... ok
test implemented_symbols_have_abi_exports ... ok
test census_is_deterministic ... ok

test result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 0 filtered out; finished in 0.16s

=== Stub Guard (bd-1h4) ===

--- Check 1: No todo!/unimplemented!/panic! in ABI layer ---
PASS: No todo!/unimplemented!/panic! in ABI crate

--- Check 2: Implemented symbols have no reachable stubs ---
PASS: No Implemented/RawSyscall symbols have reachable stubs

--- Check 3: Support matrix consistency ---
WARNING: 11 support matrix inconsistencies:
  [HIGH] freeaddrinfo: matrix says Stub, actually Implemented
    Evidence: resolv_abi.rs has full extern "C" implementation
  [HIGH] gai_strerror: matrix says Stub, actually Implemented
    Evidence: resolv_abi.rs has full extern "C" implementation
  [HIGH] getaddrinfo: matrix says Stub, actually Implemented
    Evidence: resolv_abi.rs has full extern "C" implementation
  [HIGH] getnameinfo: matrix says Stub, actually Implemented
    Evidence: resolv_abi.rs has full extern "C" implementation
  [MEDIUM] pthread_create: matrix says GlibcCallThrough, actually Implemented
    Evidence: pthread_abi.rs uses std::thread (not libc call-through)
  [MEDIUM] pthread_detach: matrix says GlibcCallThrough, actually Implemented
    Evidence: pthread_abi.rs uses std::thread (not libc call-through)
  [MEDIUM] pthread_equal: matrix says GlibcCallThrough, actually Implemented
    Evidence: pthread_abi.rs uses pure Rust (no libc:: calls)
  [MEDIUM] pthread_join: matrix says GlibcCallThrough, actually Implemented
    Evidence: pthread_abi.rs uses std::thread (not libc call-through)
  [MEDIUM] pthread_self: matrix says GlibcCallThrough, actually Implemented
    Evidence: pthread_abi.rs uses pure Rust (no libc:: calls)
  [MEDIUM] tcgetattr: matrix says RawSyscall, actually GlibcCallThrough
    Evidence: termios_abi.rs calls libc::tcgetattr()
  [MEDIUM] tcsetattr: matrix says RawSyscall, actually GlibcCallThrough
    Evidence: termios_abi.rs calls libc::tcsetattr()

--- Check 4: Stub symbols return deterministic errno ---
PASS: All exported Stub symbols return deterministic errno

=== Summary ===
Failures: 0
Warnings: 1

check_stub_guard: PASS (with 1 warning(s))

All 5 Rust tests pass, shell script passes with 0 failures. Clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:50:08.304964Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1h8","title":"EPIC: Harness integration for runtime evidence (asupersync + frankentui)","description":"Goal: make runtime_math and membrane behavior provable and debuggable via deterministic harness outputs.

Requirements (AGENTS.md):
- Use /dp/asupersync for deterministic orchestration + traceability primitives.
- Use /dp/frankentui for deterministic diff/snapshot reporting and TUI analysis.

Outputs:
- Harness commands that capture runtime_math snapshots and evidence ledgers.
- Deterministic diffs for regressions (repair rate, risk bounds, regret, etc.).
- Optional offline decode of erasure-coded evidence (if RaptorQ evidence is enabled).

Non-goal:
- Introducing these crates as runtime libc dependencies.","notes":"Status: all blocking beads are closed (snapshot capture + diff UI + regression report + evidence decode).

Harness surface (tooling-only; no runtime deps):
- `harness snapshot-kernel` (deterministic RuntimeKernelSnapshot fixture)
- `harness diff-kernel-snapshot` (plain + ftui via `frankentui-ui`)
- `harness kernel-regression-report` (strict vs hardened report)
- `harness decode-evidence` (offline evidence decode + deterministic proof/render)

Implementation anchors:
- Snapshots: `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/kernel_snapshot.rs`
- Diffs/UI: `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/snapshot_diff.rs` (+ new `evidence_decode_render.rs`)
- Evidence decode: `crates/glibc-rs-harness/src/evidence_decode.rs`

Stability fix:
- `kernel_snapshot` capture now runs on a dedicated thread with explicit stack size to avoid libtest stack overflow when debug-rendering the large `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`.

Quality gates: `cargo fmt/check/clippy/test --all-targets` are green in the current worktree.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"IndigoEagle","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.629931340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:26:17.684951624Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:26:17.684924222Z","close_reason":"Harness now provides deterministic snapshot/diff/regression + evidence decode outputs via asupersync/frankentui tooling","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-215","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-3aa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-3v3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1h8","depends_on_id":"bd-5ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":674,"issue_id":"bd-1h8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: Harness Integration (asupersync + frankentui)

### Why This Exists
Runtime math and membrane healing are only valuable if we can:
- reproduce decisions deterministically
- compare behavior vs host glibc and vs our own previous versions
- explain why a decision changed (evidence ledger)

### Hard Constraint
`/dp/asupersync` and `/dp/frankentui` are **tooling only** for glibc_rust. libc runtime must not link them.

### asupersync Role
- deterministic orchestration of conformance scenarios
- traceability primitives and stable virtual time
- scripted injection of corruption/loss patterns for evidence/FEC tests

### frankentui Role
- snapshot/diff oriented output for:
  - RuntimeKernelSnapshot
  - evidence ledger entries
  - raptorq-style decode proofs

### Artifact Contracts (Make It Mechanical)
- `RuntimeKernelSnapshot` capture format must be stable + versioned.
- Harness must be able to:
  - run scenario -> capture snapshot -> diff vs golden -> emit report
  - decode evidence stream offline -> verify -> emit proof + diff

### Definition Of Done
- One-command harness entrypoint produces deterministic report.
- Snapshot schema changes are deliberate (versioned) and show up in diffs.
- Evidence decode/proof output is readable and debuggable.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:52:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hq","title":"Kernel: Groebner normal form for violation signatures (design)","description":"Design Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization for anomaly/violation signatures.

Goal:
- Produce stable root-cause IDs from many overlapping anomaly signals.
- Avoid double-counting and improve sparse latent-cause recovery.

Design tasks:
- Define signature variables (small integer features derived from cached states).
- Define constraints/relations and what the normal form represents.
- Choose offline artifact: Groebner basis + reduction table (no heavy algebra at runtime).

Acceptance criteria:
- Concrete signature schema + deterministic reduction procedure + output ID format.","notes":"Drafted design doc: crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md (v1 atom schema A00..A24 + optional class vars C0..C5; relations/term order; offline artifact format; deterministic reduction; id format).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:32:55.351807261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:13:26.094075093Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:13:26.094056689Z","close_reason":"Drafted crates/glibc-rs-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normal_form_design.md with v1 schema, relations/term order, offline artifact sketch, deterministic reduction procedure, and gn1 id format.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Scoring dimensions defined, threshold enforced (>=2.0), scores match formula, entries have required fields.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.172285695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Opportunity matrix workflow operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_opportunity_matrix.sh gate validates scores, thresholds, and formula consistency.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf","planning"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ik","depends_on_id":"bd-26o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1isd","title":"vDSO fastpath: defensive handling for positive rc + cross-arch version strings","description":"Review of time_abi.rs vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2) flagged two minor issues that don't affect current correctness:

1) In raw_clock_gettime (line 102-105), if vdso_clock_gettime returns an unexpected POSITIVE rc (buggy vDSO or future ABI change), the current code computes set_abi_errno(-rc) which yields a negative errno value. Per POSIX errno must be positive. Fix: guard with rc < 0 check before negating, or treat any non-{0, -ENOSYS} result as a kernel anomaly warranting fallback.

2) resolve_vdso_symbols (line 166, 178) hardcodes c"LINUX_2.6" as the dlvsym version. x86_64's vDSO uses LINUX_2.6, but aarch64 uses LINUX_2.6.39 and riscv64 uses LINUX_4.15. Currently falls back to dlsym (unversioned) on non-x86_64 — works but costs a wasted dlvsym call. Fix: per-arch version constants.

Discovered during bd-j946 review.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:08:36.457319974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:41:31.897534591Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:41:31.894876280Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -478,6 +479,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-5gnk.3","title":"Standardize errno handling pattern across all ABI modules","description":"The audit found inconsistent errno handling across modules:
- Some use std::io::Error::last_os_error().raw_os_error().unwrap_or(default)
- Some use crate::host_resolve::host_errno(default)
- Some read libc::__errno_location() directly
- Some forget to set errno on error paths

STANDARD PATTERN (already used in most places):
  let e = std::io::Error::last_os_error().raw_os_error().unwrap_or(default_errno);
  unsafe { set_abi_errno(e) };

AUDIT: Grep for all error return paths (-1, NULL, EOF) and verify each sets errno.
This was partially done during the audit but a systematic pass would catch remaining gaps.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:44.276235737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:11.055032005Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:48:11.054069683Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5gnk.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5gnk","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:44.276235737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5h4xq","title":"syscall: pidfd_open nonexistent large pid should return ESRCH exactly","description":"Host glibc returns ESRCH for pidfd_open(999999999, 0). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the nonexistent-pid contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:49:42.275734753Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:02:42.291795812Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:02:42.289641116Z","close_reason":"pidfd_open(999_999_999, 0) → ESRCH pinned.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5jsr","title":"resolv: gai_suspend degenerate request sets should ignore timeout validation and preserve errno","description":"Host glibc returns EAI_ALLDONE with errno unchanged for gai_suspend on already-complete degenerate request sets even when the timeout is invalid (for example tv_nsec=1_000_000_000 or tv_sec<0). FrankenLibC currently matches that behavior, but the ABI regression suite only covers NULL timeout cases. Add focused tests and document the timeout-ignored semantics in unistd_abi.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:50:23.785286766Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:53:14.283181440Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:53:14.280876516Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity gai_suspend timeout semantics for already-complete degenerate request sets, verifying that invalid timespec values are ignored and errno stays unchanged; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-5koo6","title":"time: difftime() overflows i64 subtraction on extreme epoch pairs","description":"crates/frankenlibc-core/src/time/mod.rs difftime() computes (time1 - time0) as i64 before casting to f64. For time1 = i64::MAX, time0 = i64::MIN (or any pair whose signed difference overflows i64) this panics with 'attempt to subtract with overflow' under debug_assertions and silently wraps in release. Surfaced by fuzz_time (after fixing an unrelated antisymmetry bit-pattern assertion bug in the harness). Fix: convert both inputs to f64 first — matches glibc, f64 has enough dynamic range to represent the difference.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:28:17.805865762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T08:28:58.875548951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T08:28:58.873472189Z","close_reason":"difftime now widens to f64 before subtraction. fuzz_time harness fixed (antisymmetry via value equality not bit-pattern). 2.6M-exec fuzz pass clean. Regression test difftime_does_not_overflow_on_extreme_epochs added. Committed 4bf2c8d.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-5ky","title":"Harness: Regression report for strict vs hardened (latency/risk Pareto)","description":"Create a report that summarizes tradeoffs.

 {"id":"bd-5rs1","title":"syscall: fsmount invalid fsfd should preserve unprivileged EPERM host contract","description":"On the current host, unprivileged fsmount(-1, 0, 0) and fsmount(-1, 1, 0) fail with EPERM rather than EBADF/EINVAL. Add focused ABI parity coverage so this privilege-first behavior stays explicit.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:49:55.553436878Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.613206683Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:56:26.611053276Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-faew","title":"Exit-time segfault in LD_PRELOAD mode","notes":"ROOT CAUSE FOUND: glibc's internal _IO_list_all not patched during LD_PRELOAD. Binaries with COPY relocations work; others crash. glibc's exit path (_IO_flush_all_lockp) iterates its own _IO_list_all with corrupted vtables. FIX: dlvsym glibc's _IO_list_all at startup and set to NULL or our valid list. Confirmed _exit bypasses crash, main-return crashes.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-17T23:45:37.604034170Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T00:42:28.505593885Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T00:42:28.501510444Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-g2bd5","title":"[CRITICAL] glibc_internal_abi_test heap corruption under default parallelism (free(): invalid size)","description":"## Symptom

`rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` aborts with:

```
free(): invalid size
error: test failed, to rerun pass `-p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test`
...
process didn't exit successfully: ...glibc_internal_abi_test-... (signal: 6, SIGABRT)
```

Reproduced on two independent workers (vmi1149989, vmi1156319) with current main tip 7c9b3127 on 2026-04-22.

## Key diagnostic

The same binary with `--test-threads=1` **passes all 172 tests cleanly** (finishes in 0.04s). Corruption only manifests when the default rayon/cargo test parallelism is enabled. That narrows the bug to:

- a data race on a heap-allocated global / thread-local
- two tests freeing the same allocation through different paths
- or a reallocation that trusts a size field modified concurrently

## Location hint

Failure in the two runs landed near these test names (the test that actually freed the corrupt pointer is not necessarily the one that emitted the abort — whichever thread allocated last is what glibc trips on):

run 1 (vmi1149989): last 'ok' line was `resolv_context_native_shim_reuses_tls_context_and_preserves_errno`, then `free(): invalid size`.

run 2 (vmi1156319): last 'ok' line was `test_idna_from_dns_null_input`, then `free(): invalid size`.

Cluster of tests running around the failure: TLS dtors (`test_call_tls_dtors_*`), IDNA (`test_idna_*`), resolv context shims (`resolv_context_native_shim_*`, `resolv_context_override_*`). These all touch thread-local state / TLS dtor lists and share a registry.

## Fix direction

1. Rerun with `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 MALLOC_CHECK_=2` to get a glibc backtrace pointing at the freeing site.
2. Audit the TLS-dtor list, resolv-context override pool, and IDNA allocation paths for any `Box::from_raw` / `free` on a pointer whose size metadata is modified elsewhere without a lock.
3. Add `#[ignore]` or `serial_test::serial` gate on the implicated tests only after the root cause is understood (applying `serial` prematurely masks the bug instead of fixing it).

## Blast radius

Blocks `cargo test --workspace` from completing cleanly — the very first workspace-wide test abort halts CI before later crates (frankenlibc-core, frankenlibc-membrane, frankenlibc-harness, frankenlibc-bench) ever run. Caught by cc-libc FULL REVIEW MODE pass.

## Verification

- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test` reproduces reliably on two distinct workers.
- `cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test glibc_internal_abi_test -- --test-threads=1` passes (172/172, 0.04s).
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean.

Representative failure signatures:
- error[E0559]: variant `Ast::Repeat` has no field named `greedy`
- error[E0560]: struct `CompiledRegex` has no field named `last_error`
- error[E0560]: struct `CompiledRegex` has no field named `pattern`

+{"id":"bd-locl2","title":"FIX: fl::newlocale should reject category_mask with LC_ALL bit (DISC-LOCALE-001)","description":"POSIX 2008 newlocale: category_mask must be a bitwise OR of LC_*_MASK constants. The LC_ALL bit (1<<6 = 64) is not a valid mask bit; glibc returns NULL+EINVAL when it is set. fl::newlocale currently accepts it and returns a non-null locale_t. Fix: validate category_mask & ~LC_ALL_MASK_VALID == 0 in locale_abi.rs newlocale.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","locale"]}

 {"id":"bd-luc3d","title":"printf_conformance executor ignores length modifiers (%Lf/%td/%zd/%jd); causes varargs width mismatch","description":"crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs run_host_snprintf takes PrintfArg::Int and passes c_int to libc::snprintf, but when the format specifier uses length modifiers %td (ptrdiff_t), %zd (size_t), %jd (intmax_t), %ld, %lld, glibc reads the arg at the wider width and gets garbage upper bits. Fix: when parsing args, inspect the format string for length modifiers and promote numeric args to PrintfArg::Long before dispatch. Surfaced by sprintf_t_d in bd-12hh matrix (expected '-12345', host returned '4294954951' due to stack-read overflow).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:32:32.945225484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:07:02.859074014Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:07:02.856763492Z","close_reason":"promote_args_by_format() scans fmt specifiers and promotes Int->Long/Ulong where length modifier demands 64-bit. 8 integer length-modifier cases removed from KNOWN_IMPL_GAPS. %Lf/%Le remain skipped (f80 has no Rust equivalent). Committed d7ee870.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-tmio2","title":"FIX: tcgetattr/tcsetattr should use TCGETS2/TCSETS2 to preserve CIBAUD","description":"Discovered by termios.h conformance harness (DISC-TERMIOS-001). Modern glibc uses TCGETS2 which preserves CIBAUD (input baud) bits in c_cflag (mask 0x100f0000); fl currently uses TCGETS which loses them. Fix: switch to TCGETS2 with struct termios2 and copy fields back to user's struct termios. Affects: termios_abi.rs:561 tcgetattr, ~620 tcsetattr.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:11:30.128095196Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","conformance","termios"]}

-{"id":"bd-tmio2","title":"FIX: tcgetattr/tcsetattr should use TCGETS2/TCSETS2 to preserve CIBAUD","description":"Discovered by termios.h conformance harness (DISC-TERMIOS-001). Modern glibc uses TCGETS2 which preserves CIBAUD (input baud) bits in c_cflag (mask 0x100f0000); fl currently uses TCGETS which loses them. Fix: switch to TCGETS2 with struct termios2 and copy fields back to user's struct termios. Affects: termios_abi.rs:561 tcgetattr, ~620 tcsetattr.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T09:30:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T09:30:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","termios","conformance"]}

-{"id":"bd-locl1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc locale-aware _l function variants","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 8 functions: isalpha_l/isdigit_l/isspace_l/toupper_l/tolower_l (exhaustive 0..=255 + EOF in C locale), strtol_l (11 numeric cases), strtod_l (12 cases including inf/nan/garbage), newlocale (valid C and garbage names). Discovered DISC-LOCALE-001: fl::newlocale accepts category_mask with the LC_ALL bit set; glibc rejects it. Documented; not failed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; DISC-LOCALE-001 documented; bd-locl2 opened for fl::newlocale mask validation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","locale"]}
-{"id":"bd-locl2","title":"FIX: fl::newlocale should reject category_mask with LC_ALL bit (DISC-LOCALE-001)","description":"POSIX 2008 newlocale: category_mask must be a bitwise OR of LC_*_MASK constants. The LC_ALL bit (1<<6 = 64) is not a valid mask bit; glibc returns NULL+EINVAL when it is set. fl::newlocale currently accepts it and returns a non-null locale_t. Fix: validate category_mask & ~LC_ALL_MASK_VALID == 0 in locale_abi.rs newlocale.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:00:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","locale","conformance"]}
+{"id":"bd-errh1","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc err.h diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped (see commit). 3 functions (warnx, warn, errx — the v* and *(eval) variants share their cores). Tests: warnx body matching, warnx with %s/%d args, warn errno-suffix (5 errnos), errx fork-isolated exit-code+body (3 cases). Discovered DISC-ERR-001: fl reads progname from /proc/self/comm (truncated to TASK_COMM_LEN=15); glibc reads argv[0] basename. Logged not failed; bd-errh2 opened.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00.000000000Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; DISC-ERR-001 documented.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential","err"]}
+{"id":"bd-errh2","title":"FIX: err.h progname source — use argv[0] basename, not /proc/self/comm (DISC-ERR-001)","description":"Discovered by err.h diff harness. fl::err_abi::get_progname() reads /proc/self/comm which the kernel truncates to TASK_COMM_LEN=15 chars and which is per-task (forked children inherit, then any prctl(PR_SET_NAME) shifts it). glibc reads __progname which is the basename of argv[0] — full executable name. Result: fl warns/errs print 'conformance_dif: ...' instead of 'conformance_diff_err_h-30ba6209...: ...'. Fix: capture argv[0] in __libc_start_main (already done elsewhere?), expose as a global, basename-strip in get_progname.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00.000000000Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T10:15:00.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bug","err","conformance"]}
